```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager")) {install.packages("BiocManager")}
BiocManager::install("DESeq2", force = TRUE)
BiocManager::install("tximport", force=TRUE)
BiocManager::install(c("pathview", force = TRUE))
BiocManager::install(c("KEGGREST", force = TRUE))

if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse")}
if (!require("RColorBrewer")) {install.packages("RColorBrewer")}
if (!require("ggplot2")) {install.packages("ggplot2")}
if (!require("tidyverse")) {install.packages("tidyverse")}
if (!require("reshape2")) {install.packages("reshape2")}
if (!require("dplyr")) {install.packages("dplyr")}
if (!require("vegan")) {install.packages("vegan")}
if (!require("viridis")) {install.packages("viridis")}

library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(tximport)
library(tidyverse)
library(reshape2)
library(vegan)
library(pathview)
library(KEGGREST)
library(viridis)

packageversion("DESeq2")

```

Chunk 1 - Bring in RNAseq Data
```{r}
#!##!$$!#!#!$!# WARNING!!! If you find that there are weird errors, make sure the RMarkdown file has the right directory

# Set the working directory where your salmon_quant files are located
setwd("C:/Users/PATH/salmon_quant_results_10_1_25")

# directory is structured so that gene and isoform files for each sample are in an individual folder - only use gene level info

dir <- "."

getwd()


# import sample information table (prefix of sample [the * in *.genes.results] and the desired name [W37_1 for 1A-W37_S1])
# also has metadata

sample_info <- read.table("sample_info.txt", header = T)

#ridiculous error with above code at one point - use below to fix
# sample_info <- read.table(file.choose(), header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

# create a path/reference/link (not sure about the term) to the gene results files
# check that the files exist - output should be TRUE
# give shorter name to each file

gene_results <- file.path(dir, sample_info$folder, paste0(sample_info$name, "_quant.sf"))

#if this says FALSE, check your file names and check that the working directory of the RMarkdown file is correct
all(file.exists(gene_results))
                         

#assigning nicknames
names(gene_results) <- paste0(sample_info$name)

```


Chunk 2 - Bring in Metadata
```{r}
################ Importing salmon count data ##################
  

# Set the working directory where your salmon_quant files are located
setwd("C:/Users/PATH/salmon_quant_results_10_1_25")


#take the datasets and import them for DESeq2
#     numbers should pop up equivalent to the number of files you have
  #txOut = TRUE - doesn't summarize to gene level - needed for RSEM, but not here
txi_gene_comparison <- tximport(gene_results, type = "salmon", txOut = TRUE)

# salmon may output effective length of a gene as 0 (if not expressed in any sample)
  # report the features where this occurs
  # This chunk of code checks for 0 length 

  lengths <- as.data.frame(txi_gene_comparison$length)
  features_0 <- lengths %>% filter_all(any_vars(. %in% c(0)))
  # write.csv(features_0, paste0("features_estlength_0.csv"))
  
    # replace any 0 with imputed value, as per Mike Love's suggestion: https://support.bioconductor.org/p/92763/
  
#Skipped 4/24/25 due to no length 0 issue 
  
  #day1_txi_gene_comparison$length[day1_txi_gene_comparison$length == 0] <- NA
  
 # length_imp <- impute.knn(day1_txi_gene_comparison$length)
  #day1_txi_gene_comparison$length <- length_imp$data
  
#In the file generated below:
    #counts = estimated raw counts/expected counts from RSEM
    #abundance = TPM or FPKM
    #length = effective transcript/gene length 
  
  ###       write.csv(entire_txi_gene_comparison, "whole_dataset_txi_gene_comparison_combined.csv")


################ Importing metadata ##################

  DESeq2_table <- as.data.frame(sample_info)
  DESeq2_table <- subset(DESeq2_table, select = -c(sample, folder, name))
  rownames(DESeq2_table) <- sample_info$name
 
  
  #confirm the rownames match for the two objects 
  all(colnames(txi_gene_comparison$counts) %in% rownames(DESeq2_table))
  
  #confirm the two objects are in the same order
  all(colnames(txi_gene_comparison$counts) == rownames(DESeq2_table))
  
  
```


Chunk 3 - Bring in Eggnog Annotation Data
```{r}
# as it was written... bringing in the functional annotation information - MUST RUN CHUNK TOGETHER SINCE USING DIFFERENT WD

# # # # !!!!!!!!!!!!! before importing the .tsv files, delete all extra rows at the start and at the end of the files in excel so that the same columns are shared 

# Set the working directory where your .tsv files are located
setwd("C:/Users/PATH/prokka_MAG_annot")

#bring in annotation data 
eggnog_annot <- read.csv("eggnog_annot_from_prokka.csv", header = TRUE)


# 67,791 genes predicted with functional annotations 10/1/25

```


Chunk 4 - Running DESeq2
```{r}
#############################################################
############################################################# 
############## Running DESeq2 with one timepoint ############
#############################################################
############################################################# 

#        Metadata = DESeq2_table
#     Annotations = merged_eggnog_diamond_top_hit
# Expression data = txi_gene_comparison


# Create the DESeq2 object 
  #note: warning about characters in the formula is normal
  # want to use full metadata object for this 
entire_dds <- DESeqDataSetFromTximport(txi_gene_comparison, DESeq2_table, design = ~ day_desiled + extraction_date)


## Pruning data ## - SKIPPING FOR NOW

# Filter out genes with fewer than 10 reads across all samples
  # 270,158 genes to remove
###n_genes_before <- nrow(entire_dds)
###entire_dds_pruned <- entire_dds[rowSums(counts(entire_dds)) >= 30, ]
###n_genes_after <- nrow(entire_dds_pruned)

###genes_removed <- n_genes_before - n_genes_after
###cat("Number of genes removed:", genes_removed, "\n")

# # #  # #  # #  # #  # #  # #  #  # #  #  # #  # #

## Below are sanity checks ##

# ensure that the unused factors are dropped after subsetting
colData(entire_dds) <- droplevels(colData(entire_dds))

# ensure that all the timepoints are being noticed
  #should have 6 columns
table(entire_dds$day_desiled)


## checking design_matrix before running DESeq2 ##

# option to check the design matrix manually to ensure it worked properly
  #note: day_desiled is removed from the model 
  #should be the subset metadata file
design_matrix <- model.matrix(~day_desiled + extraction_date, data=DESeq2_table)
print(design_matrix)


## Applying updated design to subset DESeq object ##

  design(entire_dds) <- formula(~day_desiled + extraction_date)
  
## Running DESeq2 ## 
  
 dds_analyzed <- DESeq(entire_dds)
  
#Notes about the DESeq() command

#DESeq2 uses the "Relative Log Expression" (RLE) method to calculate sample-wise size factors that acoount for read depth and library composition
# assumes a negative binomial distribution
# assumes that most genes are not differentially expressed
# assumes that the probability of a read mapping to a specific gene is the same for all smaples within the same group
#   The command DESEq() strings together the following:
# estimateSizeFactors() = Normalization -> median of ratios method
# estimateDispersions() = Dispersion
# nbinomialWaldTest() = fit generalized linear model (GLM) and computes log 2 fold changes - Wald Test

#save the normalized read counts - counts from DESeq2's normalization method
# NOTE: chose to use TPM for figures instead of these values because TPM adjusts for gene length while these counts do not
  #HOWEVER, these are normalized between samples whereas TPM is only within sample normalization 

normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_dds_analyzed_10_1_25.csv')

#Merge with eggnog annotation data
  # all.x = TRUE keeps all CDS used during mapping, regardless if they received a functional annotation or not
merged_results <- merge(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, eggnog_annot, by.x="row.names", by.y="query", all.x=TRUE)

write.csv(merged_results, 'merged_results_DESeq2_normalized_counts_eggnog_annot.csv')

```


Chunk 5 - # of genes detected per genome
```{r}
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_for_detection <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_graph.csv", header = TRUE)

#counting the number of genes for each MAG used in DESeq2
norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  mutate(gene_count = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

#summarize
gene_count_summary <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  distinct(MAG, gene_count)

#set alphabetical order
mag_order <- c( "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40", "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60", "Enterococcus_sp.bin27", "Erwinia_aphidicola.bin20", "Fructilactobacillus_sp.bin56", "Fructobacillus_fructosus.bin4", "Hafnia_alvei.bin51", "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63", "Lactiplantibacillus_plantarum.bin38", "Lactococcus_lactis.bin80", "Lactococcus_taiwanensis.bin97", "Lapidilactobacillus_dextrinicus.bin55", "Latilactobacillus_curvatus.bin12", "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25", "Leclercia_pneumoniae.bin101", "Leuconostoc_citreum.bin5", "Leuconostoc_pseudomesenteroides.bin33", "Levilactobacillus_brevis.bin31", "Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9", "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96", "Pediococcus_pentosaceus.bin94", "Pseudomonas_coleopterorum.bin23", "Pseudomonas_oryzihabitans.bin50", "Rosenbergiella_epipactidis.bin100", "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93", "Variovorax_sp024372625.bin42", "Weissella_cibaria.bin45", "Weissella_sagaensis.bin34")

#apply to the dataframe
gene_count_summary$MAG <- factor(gene_count_summary$MAG, levels = rev(mag_order))


#bargraph distplaying number of genes per MAG
ggplot(gene_count_summary, aes(x = gene_count, y = MAG)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#9CAF88") +
  labs(x = "Number of Genes", y = "MAG", title = "Number of Genes per MAG") 

```

Chunk 6 - % of genes detected by MAG over time
```{r}

# using same file prepared above: norm_counts_for_detection
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 

#making binary columns

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day1_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day1_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day1_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day1_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day1_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day1_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day3_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day3_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day3_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day3_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day3_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day3_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day5_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day5_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day5_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day5_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day5_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day5_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day7_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day7_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day7_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day7_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day7_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day7_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day14_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day14_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day14_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day14_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day14_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day14_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day32_rep1_binary = if_else(UnT_Day32_rep1 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day32_rep2_binary = if_else(UnT_Day32_rep2 != 0, 1, 0))

norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(UnT_Day32_rep3_binary = if_else(UnT_Day32_rep3 != 0, 1, 0))


#simplify the dataframe
binary_norm_counts_for_detection <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  select(MAG, gene_count, ends_with("_binary"))

#getting the sum of the binary 
summed_binary <- binary_norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(across(ends_with("_binary"), sum, .names = "{.col}_sum"),
            gene_count = first(gene_count))  # keep one gene_count per MAG

#

#calculating % of genes detected 
percent_binary <- summed_binary %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_binary_sum"), 
                ~ (.x / gene_count) * 100,
                .names = "{.col}_pct"))

#simplify the dataframe
percent_binary <- percent_binary %>%
  select(MAG, ends_with("_pct"))


# Convert from wide to long
percent_long <- percent_binary %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -MAG, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

#export and add a column manually that specifies the date 
write.csv(percent_long, "percent_long.csv", row.names=FALSE)

percent_long <- read.csv("percent_long_edited.csv", header=TRUE)

#

#summarize mean and SD per MAG and day
percent_summary <- percent_long %>%
  group_by(MAG, day_desiled) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )



#set alphabetical order
mag_order <- c( "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40", "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60", "Enterococcus_sp.bin27", "Erwinia_aphidicola.bin20", "Fructilactobacillus_sp.bin56", "Fructobacillus_fructosus.bin4", "Hafnia_alvei.bin51", "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63", "Lactiplantibacillus_plantarum.bin38", "Lactococcus_lactis.bin80", "Lactococcus_taiwanensis.bin97", "Lapidilactobacillus_dextrinicus.bin55", "Latilactobacillus_curvatus.bin12", "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25", "Leclercia_pneumoniae.bin101", "Leuconostoc_citreum.bin5", "Leuconostoc_pseudomesenteroides.bin33", "Levilactobacillus_brevis.bin31", "Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9", "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96", "Pediococcus_pentosaceus.bin94", "Pseudomonas_coleopterorum.bin23", "Pseudomonas_oryzihabitans.bin50", "Rosenbergiella_epipactidis.bin100", "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93", "Variovorax_sp024372625.bin42", "Weissella_cibaria.bin45", "Weissella_sagaensis.bin34")

#apply to the dataframe
percent_summary$MAG <- factor(percent_summary$MAG, levels = rev(mag_order))

#organizing timepoints
day_order <- c("Day_1", "Day_3", "Day_5", "Day_7", "Day_14", "Day_32")

#apply to the dataframe
percent_summary$day_desiled <- factor(percent_summary$day_desiled, levels = (day_order))


ggplot(percent_summary, aes(x = mean_value, y = MAG)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") + 
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_value - sd_value, xmax = mean_value + sd_value), height = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ day_desiled, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +  
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    x = "Percentage of Genes Detected",
    y = "MAG",
    title = "Gene Detection Across Timepoints"
  ) 


```

Chunk 7 - RA of MAG over time
```{r}
# using same file prepared above: norm_counts_for_detection
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_for_detection <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_graph.csv", header = TRUE)

#calculating relative abundance per rep
norm_counts_percent <- norm_counts_for_detection %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("UnT"), ~ .x / sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE) * 100))

#calculating the relabund per MAG

# select only UnT columns
unt_cols <- grep("^UnT", names(norm_counts_percent), value = TRUE)

# sum percentages per MAG
unt_by_MAG <- norm_counts_percent %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(unt_cols), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# Convert from wide to long
percent_long <- unt_by_MAG %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -MAG, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

write.csv(percent_long, "percent_long_RA.csv", row.names = FALSE)

percent_long_RA <- read.csv("percent_long_RA_edited.csv", header=TRUE)

#get mean and sd per MAG and day
percent_summary <- percent_long_RA %>%
  group_by(MAG, day_desiled) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#order of MAGs -list made earlier
percent_summary$MAG <- factor(percent_summary$MAG, levels = rev(mag_order))


#order for timepoints - list made
percent_summary$day_desiled <- factor(percent_summary$day_desiled, levels = day_order)

ggplot(percent_summary, aes(x = mean_value, y = MAG)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#E89AA8") + 
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_value - sd_value, xmax = mean_value + sd_value), height = 0.2) +
  #facet_wrap(~ day_desiled, scales = "fixed", nrow = 1) +  
  labs(
    x = "Relative Abundance (%)",
    y = "MAG",
    title = "Relative Abundance of Normalized Transcript Counts"
  ) 

################################################################################
################################################################################
################################################################################

#that same plot but instead the RA per MAG across all timepoint


#get mean and sd per MAG 
percent_summary_all_timepoints <- percent_long_RA %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#order of MAGs -list made earlier
percent_summary_all_timepoints$MAG <- factor(percent_summary_all_timepoints$MAG, levels = rev(mag_order))

ggplot(percent_summary_all_timepoints, aes(x = mean_value, y = MAG)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#8F8193") + 
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_value - sd_value, xmax = mean_value + sd_value), height = 0.2) +
  #facet_wrap(~, scales = "fixed", nrow = 1) +  
  labs(
    x = "Relative Abundance (%)",
    y = "MAG"
  ) 

```

Chunk 8 - Homo/Heterofermentative pathways  
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)

#split the ko values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(KEGG_ko, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
homo_hetero_fermentative_ko <- read.csv("hom_hetero_fermentative_ko.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
  # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
eggnog_homo_hetero_ferment <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(KEGG_ko %in% homo_hetero_fermentative_ko$KEGG_KO)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
 # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
#combine annotation and norm count data
homo_hetero_ferment_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_homo_hetero_ferment, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment <- homo_hetero_ferment_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, KEGG_ko) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()


#append description back on 
summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- merge(summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg, homo_hetero_fermentative_ko, by.x="KEGG_ko", by.y="KEGG_KO", all.x=TRUE)


#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- summed_data_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>% select(KEGG_ko, MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_homo_hetero_ferment_long_raw <- short_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_homo_hetero_ferment_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_homo_hetero_ferment_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_homo_hetero_ferment_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_homo_hetero_ferment_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################


#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_homo_hetero_ferment_avg <- short_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_data_long <- log10_short_homo_hetero_ferment_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

#set order of description - according to pathway logic
description_order <- c(
  "Glucokinase (glk)",
  "Glucose-6-phosphate isomerase (pgi)",
  "6-phosphofructokinase 1 (pfkA)",
  "6-phosphofructokinase 2 (pfkB)",
  "Fructose-bisphosphate aldolase, class II (fbaA)",
  "Fructose-bisphosphate aldolase, class I (fbaB)",
  "Triosephosphate isomerase (tpiA)",
  "Glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase (gapA)",
  "Phosphoglycerate kinase (pgk)",
  "2,3-bisphosphoglycerate-dependent phosphoglycerate mutase (gpmA)",
  "2,3-bisphosphoglycerate-independent phosphoglycerate mutase (gpmI)",
  "Enolase (eno)",
  "Pyruvate kinase (pyk)",
  "L-lactate dehydrogenase (ldh)",
  "D-lactate dehydrogenase (ldhD)",
  "Glucose-6-phosphate 1-dehydrogenase (zwf)",
  "6-phosphogluconolactonase (pgl)",
  "6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase (gntZ/gnd)",
  "ribulose-phosphate 3-epimerase (rpe)",
  "L-ribulose-5-phosphate 4-epimerase (araD)",
  "Xylulose-5-phosphate/Fructose-6-phosphate phosphoketolase (xfp)",
  "Phosphate acetyltransferase (pta)",
  "Acetaldehyde dehydrogenase (adhE)",
  "Alcohol dehydrogenase (adh)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")



################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 6),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4, 6),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4", "6"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin55_full <- avg_data_bin55_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin55_full <- avg_data_bin55_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin55_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 6),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4, 6),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4", "6"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Lapidilactobacillus_dextrinicus.bin55",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin100_full <- avg_data_bin100_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin100_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 6),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4, 6),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4", "6"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Rosenbergiella_epipactidis.bin100",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

  #Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_bin93_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_bin93_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap

  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 6),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4, 6),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4", "6"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
```

Chunk 9 - Peptidases/proteases/exopeptidases
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
 # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
#combine annotation and norm count data
norm_count_annot <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_annot_subset, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#removing rows where there isn't and EC assigned
norm_count_annot <- norm_count_annot[norm_count_annot$EC != "-", ]

#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
norm_count_annot <- norm_count_annot %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and EC
summed_norm_count_annot <- norm_count_annot %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # 

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)


#filtering so that only peptidases remain
only_3.4_summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- short_summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  filter(str_starts(EC, "3.4"))

#getting list of unique peptidase EC
#there are 60 peptidases
list_of_peptidases_3.4_in_MAGs <- unique(only_3.4_summed_norm_count_annot_avg$EC)

#filtering so that only aminopeptidases remain
only_3.4.11_summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- short_summed_norm_count_annot_avg %>%
  filter(str_starts(EC, "3.4.11"))

#getting list of unique peptidase EC
#there are 11 aminopeptidases
only_3.4.11_df <- data.frame(EC = unique(only_3.4.11_summed_norm_count_annot_avg$EC))

#export the list, add Descriptions by hand, resume
###write.csv(only_3.4.11_df, "only_3.4.11_df.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#bring back in
description_3.4.11 <- read.csv("only_3.4.11_df.csv", header = TRUE)

#add descriptions
only_3.4.11_summed_norm_count_annot_avg <- merge(only_3.4.11_summed_norm_count_annot_avg, description_3.4.11, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#

#filtering so that only carboxypeptidase remain

  filter(str_starts(EC, "3.4.16"))

#getting list of unique peptidase EC
#there are onl 1

# # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # 

#export the list, add Descriptions by hand and combine, resume
###write.csv(only_3.4.11_df, "only_3.4.11_df.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#bring back in
##description_exopeptidases <- read.csv("exopeptidase_gang_1_1_25.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
exopeptiase_EC <- read.csv("exopeptidase_gang_1_1_25.csv", header = TRUE)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_exopeptidase_synthesis <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% exopeptiase_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#combine annotation and norm count data
exopeptidase_synthesis_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_exopeptidase_synthesis, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis <- exopeptidase_synthesis_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()


#append description back on 
summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- merge(summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg, exopeptiase_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)


#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg, Exopeptidase.group)


################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_exopeptidase_long_raw <- short_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_exopeptidase_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_exopeptidase_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#sum by Exopeptidase group

summed_by_group_data_exopeptidase_synthesis <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Exopeptidase.group) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_group_data_exopeptidase_synthesis, "order_exopeptidase_group_raw.csv", row.names=FALSE)

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

aminopeptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Aminopeptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

aminopeptidase_data_by_MAG <- aminopeptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

carboxypeptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Carboxypeptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

carboxypeptidase_data_by_MAG <- carboxypeptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

dipeptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Dipeptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

dipeptidase_data_by_MAG <- dipeptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

avg_exopeptidase_long_raw$Exopeptidase.group <- gsub("-", "_", avg_exopeptidase_long_raw$Exopeptidase.group )

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

Peptidyl_dipeptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Peptidyl_dipeptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

Peptidyl_dipeptidase_data_by_MAG <- Peptidyl_dipeptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))


#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

tripeptidyl_peptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Tripeptidyl_peptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

tripeptidyl_peptidase_data_by_MAG <- tripeptidyl_peptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))


#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

dipeptidyl_peptidase_data <- avg_exopeptidase_long_raw %>% filter(Exopeptidase.group == "Dipeptidyl_peptidase")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

dipeptidyl_peptidase_data_by_MAG <- dipeptidyl_peptidase_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))



################################################################################
################################################################################

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg <- short_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_exo_data_long <- log10_short_exopeptidase_synthesis_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )
################################################################################
################################################################################

################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Tripeptide aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.4)",
  "Methionyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.18)",
  "Membrane alanyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.2)",
  "Prolyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.5)",
  "Xaa-Pro aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.9)",
  "Glutamyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.7)",
  "PepB aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.23)",
  "Leucyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.1)",
  "Aspartyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.21)",
  "Cytosol alanyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.14)",
  "D-stereospecific aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.19)",
  "Serine-type D-Ala-D-Ala carboxypeptidase (EC 3.4.16.4)",
  "Zinc D-Ala-D-Ala carboxypeptidase (EC 3.4.17.14)",
  "Carboxypeptidase 1 (EC 3.4.17.19)",
  "Muramoyltetrapeptide carboxypeptidase (EC 3.4.17.13)",
  "Glutamate carboxypeptidase (EC 3.4.17.11)",
  "Xaa-Pro dipeptidase (EC 3.4.13.9)",
  "D-Ala-D-Ala dipeptidase (EC 3.4.13.22)",
  "Dipeptidase E (EC 3.4.13.21)",
  "Dehydropeptidase I (EC 3.4.13.19)",
  "Xaa-Pro dipeptidyl-peptidase (EC 3.4.14.11)",
  "Gamma-D-glutamyl-L-lysine dipeptidyl-peptidase (EC 3.4.14.13)",
  "Dipeptidyl-peptidase IV (EC 3.4.14.5)",
  "Glutathione gamma-glutamate hydrolase (EC 3.4.19.13)",
  "Pyroglutamyl-peptidase I (EC 3.4.19.3)",
  "Acylaminoacyl-peptidase (EC 3.4.19.1)",
  "Beta-aspartyl-peptidase (EC 3.4.19.5)",
  "Dipeptidyl carboxypeptidase (EC 3.4.15.5)",
  "Peptidyl-dipeptidase A (EC 3.4.15.1)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")


################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases <- avg_exo_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases <- avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases <- avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )

pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/exopeptidase_Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)

#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_exopeptidases, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="A")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
dev.off()

pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/exopeptidase_Lactococcus_lactis.bin80.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin80_exopeptidases, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="A")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Lactococcus_lactis.bin80",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
dev.off()

    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin94_exopeptidases <- avg_data_bin94_exopeptidases %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/exopeptidase_Pediococcus_pentosaceus.bin94.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin94_exopeptidases, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="A")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pediococcus_pentosaceus.bin94",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
dev.off()

```


Chunk 10 - Methionine synthesis (Transsulfuration/Sulfhydrylation)
```{r}


#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
methionine_synthesis_EC <- read.csv("methionine_synthesis_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
  # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
eggnog_methionine_synthesis <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% methionine_synthesis_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
 # 253 orthologs 11/6/25
#combine annotation and norm count data
methionine_synthesis_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_methionine_synthesis, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_methionine_synthesis <- methionine_synthesis_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()


#append description back on 
summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg <- merge(summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg, methionine_synthesis_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)


#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_methionine_synthesis_avg <- summed_data_methionine_synthesis_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)



#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_methionine_synthesis_avg <- short_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_meth_data_long <- log10_short_methionine_synthesis_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_methionine_synthesis <- avg_meth_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_methionine_synthesis, "order_methionine_synthesis_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
 "Aspartate aminotransferase",
  "Aspartate kinase",
  "Aspartate semialdehyde dehydrogenase",
  "Fused aspartate kinase/Homoserine dehydrogenase",
  "Homoserine O-succinyltransferase",
  "O-succinylhomoserine lyase",
  "Cysteine-S-conjugate β-lyase",
  "Homoserine O-acetyltransferase",
  "Sulfate Adenylyltransferase",
  "Adenylyl-sulfate kinase",
  "Phosphoadenylyl-sulfate reductase",
  "Assimilatory sulfite reductase (NADPH)",
  "O-acetylhomoserine aminocarboxypropyltransferase",
  "Cobalamin-dependent methionine synthase",
  "Cobalamin-independent homocysteine transmethylase"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")



################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_meth_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_meth_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin60_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin25_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Variovorax_sp024372625.bin42",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

```

Chunk 11 - Methionine degradation/catabolism 
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
methionine_degrade_EC <- read.csv("final_methionine_degradation_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
  # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
eggnog_methionine_degrade <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% methionine_degrade_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
 # 253 orthologs 11/6/25
#combine annotation and norm count data
methionine_degrade_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_methionine_degrade, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_methionine_degrade <- methionine_degrade_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()


#append description back on 
summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg <- merge(summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg, methionine_degrade_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_methionine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_methionine_degrade_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_methionine_degrade_avg <- short_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_meth_degrade_data_long <- log10_short_methionine_degrade_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_methionine_degrade <- avg_meth_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_methionine_degrade, "order_methionine_degrade_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)

#figuring out which aminopeptidase has the highest number of transcript across all timepoints

summed_by_EC_Meth_degrade_order <- avg_meth_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_EC_Meth_degrade_order, "order_by_EC_Methionine_degradation.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
 "Methionine ?-lyase (EC 4.4.1.11)",
  "S-adenosylmethionine synthetase (EC 2.5.1.6)",
  "Methionine transaminases (EC 2.6.1.88)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")



################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_meth_degrade_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin63_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

ggplot(avg_data_bin96_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="C")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
```
