

Chunk 12 - L-Lysine Synthesis
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
lysine_syn_EC <- read.csv("lysine_synthesis_11_19_25.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
  # 581 orthologs 10/6/25
eggnog_lysine_syn <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% lysine_syn_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
 # 253 orthologs 11/6/25
#combine annotation and norm count data
lysine_syn_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_lysine_syn, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_lysine_syn <- lysine_syn_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_lysine_syn_avg <- merge(summed_data_lysine_syn_avg, lysine_syn_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_lysine_syn_avg <- summed_data_lysine_syn_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_lysine_syn_avg <- short_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_lysine_syn_data_long <- log10_short_lysine_syn_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_lysine_syn <- avg_lysine_syn_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_lysine_syn, "order_lysine_syn_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Aspartate kinase (EC 2.7.2.4)",
  "Aspartate semialdehyde dehydrogenase (EC 1.2.1.11)",
  "4-hydroxy-tetrahydrodipicolinate synthase (EC 4.3.3.7)",
  "4-hydroxy-tetrahydrodipicolinate reductase (EC 1.17.1.8)",
  "2,3,4,5-tetrahydropyridine-2,6-dicarboxylate N-succinyltransferase (EC 2.3.1.117)",
  "Succinyldiaminopimelate transaminase (EC 2.6.1.17)",
  "succinyl-diaminopimelate desuccinylase (EC 3.5.1.18)",
  "Tetrahydrodipicolinate N-acetyltransferase (EC 2.3.1.89)",
  "N-acetyldiaminopimelate deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.47)",
  "Diaminopimelate epimerase (EC 5.1.1.7)",
  "Diaminopimelate decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.20)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")



################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_lysine_syn_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_lysine_syn_data_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin60_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin25_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")),  # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Variovorax_sp024372625.bin42",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),

```

Chunk 13 - L-Lysine Degradation
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
lysine_degrade_EC <- read.csv("lysine_degradation_11_20_25.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_lysine_degrade <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% lysine_degrade_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
lysine_degrade_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_lysine_degrade, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_lysine_degrade <- lysine_degrade_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg <- merge(summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg, lysine_degrade_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_lysine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_lysine_degrade_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_lysine_degrade_avg <- short_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_lysine_degrade_data_long <- log10_short_lysine_degrade_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_lysine_degrade <- avg_lysine_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_lysine_degrade, "order_lysine_degrade_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)

#figuring out which aminopeptidase has the highest number of transcript across all timepoints

summed_by_EC_lysine_degrade_order <- avg_lysine_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_EC_lysine_degrade_order, "order_by_EC_lysine_degradation.csv", row.names=FALSE)



################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Lysine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.18)",
  "Lysine 2,3-aminomutase (EC 5.4.3.2)",
  "Lysine racemase (EC 5.1.1.5)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_lysine_degrade_data_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

#set cells with 0 as NA

# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin63_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

avg_data_bin96_full <- avg_data_bin96_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin96_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="D")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4.3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

```




Chunk 14 - Histidine Synthesis
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
histidine_syn_EC <- read.csv("histidine_synthesis_11_24_25.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_histidine_syn <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% histidine_syn_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
histidine_syn_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_histidine_syn, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_histidine_syn <- histidine_syn_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_histidine_syn_avg <- merge(summed_data_histidine_syn_avg, histidine_syn_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_histidine_syn_avg <- summed_data_histidine_syn_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_histidine_syn_avg <- short_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_histidine_syn_data_long <- log10_short_histidine_syn_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_histidine_syn <- avg_histidine_syn_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_histidine_syn, "order_histidine_syn_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "ATP phosphoribosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.17)",
  "Phosphoribosyl-ATP diphosphatase (EC 3.6.1.31)",
  "Phosphoribosyl-AMP cyclohydrolase (EC 3.5.4.19)",
  "1-(5-phosphoribosyl)-5-[(5-phosphoribosylamino)methylideneamino]imidazole-4-carboxamide isomerase (EC 5.3.1.16)",
  "Imidazole glycerol-phosphate synthase (EC 4.3.2.10)",
  "Imidazoleglycerol-phosphate dehydratase (EC 4.2.1.19)",
  "Histidinol-phosphate transaminase (EC 2.6.1.9)",
  "Alkaline phosphatase (EC 3.1.3.1)",
  "Histidinol-phosphatase (EC 3.1.3.15)",
  "Histidinol dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.1.23)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################


################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_histidine_syn_data_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_histidine_syn_data_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA


#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin60_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin25_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
     limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin42_full <- avg_data_bin42_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin42_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")),  # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
     limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Variovorax_sp024372625.bin42",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

```


Chunk 14 - Histidine Synthesis
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
histidine_degrade_EC <- read.csv("histidine_degradation_11_26_25.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_histidine_degrade <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% histidine_degrade_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
histidine_degrade_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_histidine_degrade, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_histidine_degrade <- histidine_degrade_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg <- merge(summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg, histidine_degrade_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_histidine_degrade_avg <- summed_data_histidine_degrade_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_histidine_degrade_avg <- short_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_histidine_degrade_data_long <- log10_short_histidine_degrade_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_histidine_degrade <- avg_histidine_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_histidine_degrade, "order_histidine_degrade_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


#figuring out which aminopeptidase has the highest number of transcript across all timepoints

summed_by_EC_histidine_degrade_order <- avg_histidine_degrade_data_long %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_EC_histidine_degrade_order, "order_by_EC_histidine_degradation.csv", row.names=FALSE)




################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Histidine ammonia-lyase (EC 4.3.1.3)",
  "L-histidine N(alpha)-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.44)",
  "Histidine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.22)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_histidine_degrade_data_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Rosenbergiella_epipactidis.bin100",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

  #Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_histidine_degrade_data_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_bin93_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_bin93_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin93_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
   limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )




```




Chunk 15 - Biogenic Amine Formation
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

#bring in the pathway information 
amine_EC <- read.csv("biogenic_amine_formation_from_AA_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_amine <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% amine_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
amine_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_amine, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_amine <- amine_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_amine_avg <- merge(summed_data_amine_avg, amine_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_amine_avg <- summed_data_amine_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Amine, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_amine_long_raw <- short_amine_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_amine_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_amine_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_amine_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_amine_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


# 3) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_amine_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Amine) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_amine_by_Amine_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

tyramine_data <- avg_amine_long_raw %>% filter(Amine == "Tyramine")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

tyramine_data_by_MAG <- tyramine_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

Cadaverine_data <- avg_amine_long_raw %>% filter(Amine == "Cadaverine")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

Cadaverine_data_by_MAG <- Cadaverine_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

Putrescine_data <- avg_amine_long_raw %>% filter(Amine == "Putrescine")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

Putrescine_data_by_MAG <- Putrescine_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#%##$%#%#$%#%#%

Spermidine_data <- avg_amine_long_raw %>% filter(Amine == "Spermidine")

#order of MAG that make aminopeptidase

Spermidine_data_by_MAG <- Spermidine_data %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

################################################################################
################################################################################


#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_amine_avg <- short_amine_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_amine_long <- log10_short_amine_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "L-tyrosine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.25)",
  "L-lysine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.18)",
  "L-ornithine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.17)",
  "Carbamoylputrescine amidase (EC 3.5.1.53)",
  "Agmatinase (EC 3.5.3.11)",
  "Spermidine synthase (EC 2.5.1.16)",
  "Carboxynorspermidine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.96)",
  "L-histidine decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.22)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################
################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_amine_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)

avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,
    Average == 0 ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ Average
  )) %>%
  mutate(Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order)))

pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/amine_Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40.pdf",
    width = 8.5, height = 9)

ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")),
    na.value = "white",
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

dev.off()

#Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96
avg_data_bin96_full <- avg_amine_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)

avg_data_bin96_full <- avg_data_bin96_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,
    Average == 0 ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ Average
  )) %>%
  mutate(Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order)))

pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/amine_Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96.pdf",
    width = 8.5, height = 9)

ggplot(avg_data_bin96_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="turbo")),
    na.value = "white",
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pantoea_agglomerans.bin96",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

dev.off()
```

Chunk 16 - Carbon sequestration / heterotrophic CO2 assimilation
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(KEGG_ko, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
carbon_seq_ko <- read.csv("carbon_sequestration_ko.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_carbon_seq <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(KEGG_ko %in% carbon_seq_ko$KEGG_ko)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
carbon_seq_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_carbon_seq, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_carbon_seq <- carbon_seq_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, KEGG_ko) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_carbon_seq_avg <- merge(summed_data_carbon_seq_avg, carbon_seq_ko, by.x="KEGG_ko", by.y="KEGG_ko", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_carbon_seq_avg <- summed_data_carbon_seq_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, KEGG_ko, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_carbon_seq_long_raw <- short_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_carbon_seq_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_carbon_seq_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_carbon_seq_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_carbon_seq_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_carbon_seq_avg <- short_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_carbon_seq_long <- log10_short_carbon_seq_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Glycerol facilitator uptake protein (glpF)",
  "Carbamoyl-phosphate synthase large chain (carB, EC 6.3.5.5)",
  "Pyruvate carboxylase (pyc, EC 6.4.1.1)",
  "Acetyl-coenzyme A carboxylase carboxyl transferase subunit beta (accD, EC 6.4.1.2)",
  "Biotin carboxylase (accC, EC 6.3.4.14)",
  "Carbamoyl-phosphate synthase small chain (carA, EC 6.3.5.5)",
  "3-oxoacyl-[acyl-carrier-protein] synthase 3 (fabH, EC 2.3.1.180)",
  "Acetyl-coenzyme A carboxylase carboxyl transferase subunit alpha (accA, EC 6.4.1.2)",
  "Biotin carboxyl carrier protein of acetyl-CoA carboxylase (accB, EC 2.1.3.15)",
  "Carbonic anhydrase (cah, EC 4.2.1.1)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

# ----------------------------
# Output folder
# ----------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/PATH/carbon_sequestration_11_30_25"
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ----------------------------
# Function to build + save one MAG heatmap
# ----------------------------
save_mag_heatmap <- function(mag_name) {

  df <- avg_carbon_seq_long %>%
    filter(MAG == mag_name) %>%
    select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
    mutate(
      Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order)), # reverse for plotting
      Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
    ) %>%
    complete(Description, Timepoint) %>%
    mutate(
      Average_plot = case_when(
        is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> white
        Average == 0   ~ 0,          # present but zero -> grey
        TRUE           ~ Average
      )
    )

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors   = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option = "B")),
      na.value = "white",
      limits   = c(0, 5),
      breaks   = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),
      labels   = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
      name     = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid  = element_blank(),
      plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = mag_name,
      x = "Timepoint",
      y = "Description"
    )

  # Safe filename (Windows doesn’t like some characters)
  safe_mag <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9._-]", "_", mag_name)
  file_path <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("carbon_sequestration_", safe_mag, ".pdf"))

  pdf(file = file_path, width = 9.5, height = 9)
  print(p)
  dev.off()

  message("Saved: ", file_path)
}

# ----------------------------
# Run for EVERY MAG in the dataset
# ----------------------------
all_mags <- sort(unique(avg_carbon_seq_long$MAG))
purrr::walk(all_mags, save_mag_heatmap)



```


Chunk 17 - Nitrogen metabolism
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
###eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  ###mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
nitrogen_met_EC <- read.csv("nitrogen_metabolism_EC_11_30_25.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_nitrogen_met <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% nitrogen_met_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
nitrogen_met_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_nitrogen_met, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_nitrogen_met <- nitrogen_met_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg <- merge(summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg, nitrogen_met_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_nitrogen_met_avg <- summed_data_nitrogen_met_avg %>% select(MAG, EC,  Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)


################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_nitrogen_met_long_raw <- short_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_nitrogen_met_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_nitrogen_met_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_nitrogen_met_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_nitrogen_met_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
########################




#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_nitrogen_met_avg <- short_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_nitrogen_met_long <- log10_short_nitrogen_met_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Membrane-bound nitrate reductase (EC 1.7.5.1)",
  "Periplasmic nitrate reductase (EC 1.9.6.1)",
  "Nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.2.1)",
  "Nitric oxide reductase (EC 1.7.2.5)",
  "Nitrous-oxide reductase (EC 1.7.2.4)",
  "NADH nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.1.15)",
  "Cytochrome C nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.2.2)",
  "Ferredoxin nitrate reductase (EC 1.7.7.2)",
  "NADH nitrate reductase (EC 1.7.1.1)",
  "NAD(P)H nitrate reductase (EC 1.7.1.2)",
  "NAD(P)H nitrate reductase (EC 1.7.1.3)",
  "NAD(P)H nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.1.4)",
  "Ferredoxin nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.7.1)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################

  #Hafnia_alvei.bin51

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin51_full <- avg_nitrogen_met_long %>%
  filter(MAG == "Hafnia_alvei.bin51") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin51_full <- avg_data_bin51_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin51_full <- avg_data_bin51_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin51_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 3),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 2, 3),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "2", "3"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hafnia_alvei.bin51",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

```


Chunk 18 - VFA formation
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
###eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  ###mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
VFA_EC <- read.csv("VFA_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_VFA_EC <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% VFA_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
VFA_EC_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_VFA_EC, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_VFA <- VFA_EC_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_VFA_avg <- merge(summed_data_VFA_avg, VFA_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, VFA, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_VFA_long_raw <- short_VFA_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_VFA_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_VFA_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_VFA_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_VFA_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#
#

#filtering so that only acetate producing remain
only_acetate <- avg_VFA_long_raw %>%
  filter(str_detect(VFA, "Acetate"))

#gfiguring out which MAG has highest acetate producing transcription
acetate_by_MAG <- only_acetate %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE))

#export the list, add Descriptions by hand, resume
write.csv(acetate_by_MAG, "acetate_by_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################


#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_VFA_avg <- short_VFA_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_VFA_long <- log10_short_VFA_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )


################################################################################

#set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Acetate kinase (EC 2.7.2.1)",
  "Cysteine synthase (EC 2.5.1.47)",
  "Acylphosphatase (EC 3.6.1.7)",
  "Propionate CoA-transferase (EC 2.8.3.1)",
  "Acetate-CoA ligase (EC 6.2.1.1)",
  "Pyruvate dehydrogenase (EC 1.2.5.1)",
  "Aldehyde dehydrogenase (NAD+) (EC 1.2.1.3)",
  "Succinyl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase (EC 2.8.3.18)",
  "Acetyl-CoA hydrolase (EC 3.1.2.1)",
  "Butyrate kinase (EC 2.7.2.7)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################
# VFA heatmaps -> one PDF per MAG
# Output folder:
out_dir <- "C:/Users/PATH/VFA_production_12_4_25"
if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)

################################################################################
# Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40

avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_VFA_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)

avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,
    Average == 0 ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ Average
  )) %>%
  mutate(Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order)))

pdf(file = file.path(out_dir, "VFA_Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40.pdf"), width = 8.5, height = 9)

ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option = "F")),
    na.value = "white",
    limits = c(0, 4.11),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid   = element_blank(),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

dev.off()

  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)

avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,
    Average == 0 ~ 0,
    TRUE ~ Average
  )) %>%
  mutate(Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order)))

pdf(file = file.path(out_dir, "VFA_Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25.pdf"), width = 8.5, height = 9)

ggplot(avg_data_bin25_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option = "F")),
    na.value = "white",
    limits = c(0, 4.11),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid   = element_blank(),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

dev.off()
```
