    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weissella_sagaensis.bin34",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

dev.off()

################################################################################

```



Chunk 19 - Figuring out which MAG has highest number of transcripts for each VFA
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
###eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  ###mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
VFA_EC <- read.csv("VFA_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_VFA_EC <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% VFA_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
VFA_EC_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_VFA_EC, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_VFA <- VFA_EC_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_VFA_avg <- merge(summed_data_VFA_avg, VFA_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_VFA_avg <- summed_data_VFA_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_VFA_avg <- short_VFA_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_VFA_long <- log10_short_VFA_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

#figuring out who has the highest number of transcripts across all timepoints 
  #determine order for figure
  #sum by MAG

summed_by_MAG_data_VFA <- avg_VFA_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_data_VFA, "order_VFA_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


#figuring out which biogenic amine has the highest number of transcript across all timepoints

summed_by_EC_VFA <- avg_VFA_long %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_EC_VFA, "order_VFA_by_Description.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

# Acetate only

#removing non-acetate forming ECs
acetate_avg_VFA_long <- avg_VFA_long[avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.7.2.15", ]
acetate_avg_VFA_long <- acetate_avg_VFA_long[acetate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "6.2.1.17", ]
acetate_avg_VFA_long <- acetate_avg_VFA_long[acetate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.7.2.7", ]

#determining MAG with highest level of expression

summed_by_MAG_acetate_avg_VFA_long <- acetate_avg_VFA_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_acetate_avg_VFA_long, "order_acetate_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)

#$#$#$#$

# Propionate only

#removing non-propionate forming ECs
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- avg_VFA_long[avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.13.12.4", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "3.6.1.7", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.2.5.1", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.2.1.3", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.8.3.18", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "3.1.2.1", ]
propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long[propionate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.7.2.7", ]

#determining MAG with highest level of expression

summed_by_MAG_propionate_avg_VFA_long <- propionate_avg_VFA_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_propionate_avg_VFA_long, "order_propionate_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


#$#$#$#$

# BUtyrate only

#removing non-butyrate forming ECs
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- avg_VFA_long[avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.13.12.4", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.7.2.1", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "3.6.1.7", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "6.2.1.1", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.2.5.1", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "1.2.1.3", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.8.3.18", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "3.1.2.1", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "2.7.2.15", ]
butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long[butyrate_avg_VFA_long$EC != "6.2.1.17", ]

#determining MAG with highest level of expression

summed_by_MAG_butyrate_avg_VFA_long <- butyrate_avg_VFA_long %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(sum_Average = sum(Average, na.rm = TRUE))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_butyrate_avg_VFA_long, "order_butyrate_MAG.csv", row.names=FALSE)


################################################################################

#peaking at nitrogen metabolism

#Dissimilatory ntirate reduction only

#removing non-propionate forming ECs
dissim_avg_nitrogen_met_long <- avg_nitrogen_met_long[avg_nitrogen_met_long$EC != "1.7.2.1", ]
dissim_avg_nitrogen_met_long <- dissim_avg_nitrogen_met_long[dissim_avg_nitrogen_met_long$EC != "1.7.2.5", ]

Chunk 20 - Ammonia synthesis
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
###eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  ###mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
ammonia_EC <- read.csv("ammonia_making_1_6_26.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
eggnog_ammonia <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% ammonia_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
ammonia_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_ammonia, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_ammonia <- ammonia_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_ammonia_avg <- merge(summed_data_ammonia_avg, ammonia_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_ammonia_avg <- summed_data_ammonia_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_ammonia_long_raw <- short_ammonia_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_ammonia_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_ammonia_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_ammonia_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_ammonia_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_ammonia_avg <- short_ammonia_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_ammonia_long <- log10_short_ammonia_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

# set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Carbamate kinase (EC 2.7.2.2)",
  "Arginine deiminase (EC 3.5.3.6)",
  "Alanine dehydrogenase (EC 1.4.1.1)",
  "Asparaginase (EC 3.5.1.1)",
  "Amidase (EC 3.5.1.4)",
  "NADH nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.1.15)",
  "N-carbamoylputrescine amidase (EC 3.5.1.53)",
  "Glutamate dehydrogenase (NADP+) (EC 1.4.1.4)",
  "Hydroxylamine reductase (EC 1.7.99.1)",
  "Aminomethyltransferase (EC 2.1.2.10)",
  "Glutamate dehydrogenase (EC 1.4.1.2)",
  "Nitrilase (EC 3.5.5.1)",
  "Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate synthase (EC 1.4.3.5)",
  "Nicotinamidase (EC 3.5.1.19)",
  "Glutamate dehydrogenase (NADP+) (EC 1.4.1.3)",
  "Glutaminase (EC 3.5.1.2)",
  "Glutaminase-asparaginase (EC 3.5.1.38)",
  "Ferredoxin nitrite reductase (EC 1.7.7.1)",
  "Beta-ureidopropionase (EC 3.5.1.6)",
  "Leucine dehydrogenase (EC 1.4.1.9)",
  "Formamidase (EC 3.5.1.49)",
  "2,4-diaminopentanoate dehydrogenase (EC 1.4.1.12)",
  "2,4-diaminopentanoate dehydrogenase (NAD+) (EC 1.4.1.26)",
  "Urease (EC 3.5.1.5)",
  "Aspartate dehydrogenase (EC 1.4.1.21)"
)


timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################
 

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_ammonia_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/ammonia_Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)
 
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 5),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 
dev.off()
################################################################################
 
################################################################################
 
#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_ammonia_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin60_full <- avg_data_bin60_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
 pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/ammonia_Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)

  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
   limits = c(0, 5),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_mundtii.bin60",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()
################################################################################
 
################################################################################
 
#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin27_full <- avg_ammonia_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_sp.bin27") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin27_full <- avg_data_bin27_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin27_full <- avg_data_bin27_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
  pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/ammonia_Enterococcus_sp.bin27.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin27_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
   limits = c(0, 5),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_sp.bin27",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()

#adjust order
avg_data_bin25_full <- avg_data_bin25_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/ammonia_Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)  
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin25_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
   limits = c(0, 5),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Latilactobacillus_sakei.bin25",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()

    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin93_full <- avg_data_bin93_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
  pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/ammonia_Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9) 
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin93_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="E")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
   limits = c(0, 5),
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 3, 5),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "1", "3", "5"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Stenotrophomonas_sp.bin93",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()

Chunk 21 - Starch Degradation
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
####mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
starch_deg_EC <- read.csv("starch_degradation_EC.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
 
eggnog_starch_deg <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% starch_deg_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
starch_deg_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_starch_deg, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_starch_deg_data <- starch_deg_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_starch_deg_avg <- merge(summed_data_starch_deg_avg, starch_deg_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_starch_deg_avg <- summed_data_starch_deg_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_starch_long_raw <- short_starch_deg_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_starch_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_starch_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_starch_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_starch_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_starch_deg_avg <- short_starch_deg_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_starch_deg_long <- log10_short_starch_deg_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

description_order <- c(
  "Maltogenic alpha-amylase (EC 3.2.1.133)",
  "Alpha-amylase (EC 3.2.1.1)",
  "Amylophosphorylase (EC 2.4.1.1)",
  "Isoamylase (EC 3.2.1.68)",
  "Cyclomaltodextrin glucanotransferase (EC 2.4.1.19)",
  "Glucan 1,4-alpha-glucosidase (EC 3.2.1.3)"
)

timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_starch_deg_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )


#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="B")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin38_full <- avg_data_bin38_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin38_full <- avg_data_bin38_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin38_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="B")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
         limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Lactiplantibacillus_plantarum.bin38",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin45_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="B")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
        limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weissella_cibaria.bin45",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

  #Weissella_sagaensis.bin34

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin34_full <- avg_starch_deg_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Weissella_sagaensis.bin34") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA

#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin34_full <- avg_data_bin34_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))


#adjust order
avg_data_bin34_full <- avg_data_bin34_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )



#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin34_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="B")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
       limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weissella_sagaensis.bin34",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )

################################################################################

```



Chunk 22- Carbon fixing pathways (Wood-Ljungdahl and Reductive Glycine Pathway)
```{r}

#getting the normalized counts from DESeq2, done earlier 
normalized_counts_dds_analyzed <- counts(dds_analyzed, normalized = TRUE)
head(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed)

write.csv(normalized_counts_dds_analyzed, 'normalized_counts_for_detection.csv')

# using same file prepared above: log_norm_counts
  #all that I added was a column that specifies which MAG the gene came from 
norm_counts_MAG <- read.csv("normalized_counts_for_detection_edited.csv", header = TRUE)

# recall that eggnog_annot is the annotation file
  #simplifying the annotation file
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot %>% select(query, Description, Preferred_name, EC, KEGG_ko)


#split the EC values separated by "," and make a new column that appends the same values
eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  separate_rows(EC, sep = ",")

# removing the "ko:" prefix imposed by the eggnog mapper
###eggnog_annot_subset <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  ###mutate(KEGG_ko = sub("ko:", "", KEGG_ko))


#bring in the pathway information 
carbon_fix_EC <- read.csv("carbon_fix_pathways_1_19_26.csv", header=T)

# Annotation data - Keep only pathway of interest KOs
eggnog_carbon_fix <- eggnog_annot_subset %>%
  filter(EC %in% carbon_fix_EC$EC)

##############################################################
# # # # # # # # Bringing in the DESeq2 data  # # # # # # # # #
##############################################################

#filtering normalized counts file to only keep genes involved in pathway
#combine annotation and norm count data
carbon_fix_data <- merge(norm_counts_MAG, eggnog_carbon_fix, by.x="Gene", by.y="query", all.y=TRUE)

#normalized counts per replicate summed by MAG and KEGG_ko
summed_data_carbon_fix <- carbon_fix_data %>%
  group_by(MAG, EC) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("UnT"), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")



#get averages per timepoint:
summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_1_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day1_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_3_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day3_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_5_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day5_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_7_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day7_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_14_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day14_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Day_32_Avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("UnT_Day32_rep")), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

#append description back on 
summed_data_carbon_fix_avg <- merge(summed_data_carbon_fix_avg, carbon_fix_EC, by.x="EC", by.y="EC", all.x=TRUE)

#simplify the dataframe
  #simplifying the annotation file
short_carbon_fix_avg <- summed_data_carbon_fix_avg %>% select(MAG, EC, Description, Day_1_Avg, Day_3_Avg, Day_5_Avg, Day_7_Avg, Day_14_Avg, Day_32_Avg)

################################################################################
################ Top MAG and gene over time ####################################
################################################################################

# Long format from RAW averages
avg_carbon_fix_long_raw <- short_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average_raw"
  )

# 1) MAG ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_MAG_raw <- avg_carbon_fix_long_raw %>%
  group_by(MAG) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_MAG_raw, "order_carbon_fix_MAG_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 2) Gene (Description) ordering: total transcription across timepoints (raw)
summed_by_gene_raw <- avg_carbon_fix_long_raw %>%
  group_by(Description) %>%
  summarise(total_raw = sum(Average_raw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_raw))

write.csv(summed_by_gene_raw, "order_carbon_fix_by_Description_RAW.csv", row.names = FALSE)


################################################################################
################################################################################

#log10 transforming the normalized counts so that the scale isn't crazy
  #log 10 is saying - what power does 10 need to be raised to do get this value
log10_short_carbon_fix_avg <- short_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("Day"), ~ log10(.x + 1)))

#make long
avg_carbon_fix_long <- log10_short_carbon_fix_avg %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Day_"),
    names_to = "Timepoint",
    values_to = "Average"
  )

################################################################################

# set order of description
description_order <- c(
  "Formate dehydrogenase (EC 1.17.1.9)",
  "Formyltetrahydrofolate synthetase (EC 6.3.4.3)",
  "Methenyltetrahydrofolate cyclohydrolase (EC 3.5.4.9)",
  "Methylenetetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase (NADP(+)) (EC 1.5.1.5)",
  "Glycine cleavage system (EC 1.4.1.27)",
  "Glycine reductase (EC 1.21.4.2)",
  "Acetate kinase (2.7.2.1)",
  "Acetate_CoA ligase (EC 6.2.1.1)",
  "Pyruvate synthase (EC 1.2.7.1)"
)


timepoint_order <- c("Day_1_Avg", "Day_3_Avg", "Day_5_Avg", "Day_7_Avg", "Day_14_Avg", "Day_32_Avg")

################################################################################

################################################################################
 

#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_carbon_fix_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin40_full <- avg_data_bin40_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/carbon_fix_Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9)
 
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin40_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
       limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Enterococcus_faecalis.bin40",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 
dev.off()

################################################################################
 
  #Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63
 
#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_carbon_fix_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin63_full <- avg_data_bin63_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
 pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/carbon_fix_Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9) 
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin63_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
       limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Kosakonia_cowanii.bin63",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()

################################################################################
 
 #Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9
 
#CHANGE MAG - subset and reorder
avg_data_bin9_full <- avg_carbon_fix_long%>%
  filter(MAG == "Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9") %>%
  select(Description, Timepoint, Average) %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = description_order),
    Timepoint   = factor(Timepoint, levels = timepoint_order)
  ) %>%
  complete(Description, Timepoint)  # leave missing combinations as NA
 
#set cells with 0 as NA
# Create a column for plotting:
# - NA (missing gene) -> keep NA
# - 0 -> 0 (will map to grey)
# - >0 -> value (for gradient)
avg_data_bin9_full <- avg_data_bin9_full %>%
  mutate(Average_plot = case_when(
    is.na(Average) ~ NA_real_,  # missing gene -> NA
    Average == 0 ~ 0,           # present but zero -> 0
    TRUE ~ Average               # present and >0 -> actual value
  ))
 
 
#adjust order
avg_data_bin9_full <- avg_data_bin9_full %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(description_order))  # reverse for plotting
  )
 
 
 pdf(file = "C:/Users/PATH/carbon_fix_Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9.pdf",   # The directory you want to save the file in
    width = 8.5, # The width of the plot in inches
    height = 9) 
#make heatmap
ggplot(avg_data_bin9_full, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Description, fill = Average_plot)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("grey", viridisLite::viridis(100, option="G")), # grey for zero, turbo for positive
    na.value = "white",                                             # missing genes = white
        limits = c(0, 4.2),
    breaks = c(0.1, 2, 4),                                # legend ticks
    labels = c("0.1", "2", "4"),
    name = "Log10(Average Normalized Count)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Loigolactobacillus_coryniformis.bin9",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Description"
  )
 dev.off()
